phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Slight edge at level 1: your soldier-poke shuffle control beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: At level 2 the numbers lean your way — soldier-poke shuffle control edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Slight edge at level 3: your soldier-poke shuffle control beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Nashor's components arrive and the soldier DPS starts to sting. Drag Qs through Anivia on every last-hit he takes.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Slight deficit at the level-6 breakpoint: Anivia's wall-frost poke control edge out your soldier-poke shuffle control on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Nashor's spike: your soldier poke becomes shred. Shove crashes and rotate — the soldiers siege towers better than any mid.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Full build Azir is a teamfight: R cuts the fight in half, soldiers melt whoever is on your side of the wall.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: At level 1 your soldier-poke shuffle control beat the stardust ramp control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Aurelion Sol answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Your soldier-poke shuffle control out-muscle Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control at level 2 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: E completes the safety net — you now have the shield-dash out. Greedier soldier placements are allowed, not encouraged.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Slight edge through levels 4-5: your soldier-poke shuffle control beat Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: At the level-6 breakpoint the numbers lean your way — soldier-poke shuffle control edge out the stardust ramp control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Slight edge on first item: your soldier-poke shuffle control beat Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Full build Azir is a teamfight: R cuts the fight in half, soldiers melt whoever is on your side of the wall.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: W a soldier behind the wave and farm THROUGH it — your auto range is the soldier's range. Don't poke yet; sand costs mana.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Two soldiers up means a real trade: W-Q the drag-through and step back. The poke is free only when his engage is down.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: E completes the safety net — you now have the shield-dash out. Greedier soldier placements are allowed, not encouraged.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Nashor's components arrive and the soldier DPS starts to sting. Drag Qs through Azir on every last-hit he takes.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Emperor's Divide online — the dive insurance and the insec flip. With R up you win every 2v2 your jungler joins.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: W a soldier behind the wave and farm THROUGH it — your auto range is the soldier's range. Don't poke yet; sand costs mana.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Two soldiers up means a real trade: W-Q the drag-through and step back. The poke is free only when his engage is down.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: E completes the safety net — you now have the shield-dash out. Greedier soldier placements are allowed, not encouraged.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Nashor's components arrive and the soldier DPS starts to sting. Drag Qs through Veigar on every last-hit he takes.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Emperor's Divide online — the dive insurance and the insec flip. With R up you win every 2v2 your jungler joins.

phases.why: E completes the safety net — you now have the shield-dash out. Greedier soldier placements are allowed, not encouraged.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Nashor's components arrive and the soldier DPS starts to sting. Drag Qs through Irelia on every last-hit he takes.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Emperor's Divide online — the dive insurance and the insec flip. With R up you win every 2v2 your jungler joins.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Nashor's spike: your soldier poke becomes shred. Shove crashes and rotate — the soldiers siege towers better than any mid.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Full build Azir is a teamfight: R cuts the fight in half, soldiers melt whoever is on your side of the wall.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: W a soldier behind the wave and farm THROUGH it — your auto range is the soldier's range. Don't poke yet; sand costs mana.

phases.why: Two soldiers up means a real trade: W-Q the drag-through and step back. The poke is free only when his engage is down.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: E completes the safety net — you now have the shield-dash out. Greedier soldier placements are allowed, not encouraged.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Nashor's components arrive and the soldier DPS starts to sting. Drag Qs through Pantheon on every last-hit he takes.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Emperor's Divide online — the dive insurance and the insec flip. With R up you win every 2v2 your jungler joins.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Nashor's spike: your soldier poke becomes shred. Shove crashes and rotate — the soldiers siege towers better than any mid.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: At two-plus items your soldier-poke shuffle control beat the point-click spear bullying in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Pantheon answer you, not the other way around.
